behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: file discovery/parsing + ordering + execution sequencing. A pure Unit test of the sorter can miss bugs where runner iteration order differs from sorted output. REAL-INFRA does not require OS/network/subprocess beyond local filesystem and DB wiring. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires filename-prefix ordering, not real subprocess/network/LLM steps.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate Integration (chosen): setup temp migrations dir with out-of-order files (e.g., 002, 001, 003) and run against test Postgres; assert side-effect rows prove execution order 1->2->3; catches bug where discovery order leaks through. Candidate Unit (adjacent lower): setup filename list into sort function; assert sorted list; catches parser/sort bug but misses runner-loop ordering regressions.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is idempotency across runs via _migrations metadata and executor interaction. Unit-only tests can verify skip logic but miss persistence/state handoff defects between recorder and selector. No real OS/subprocess requirement in behavior text; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate Integration (chosen): run runner twice on same test DB with one migration that inserts a marker row; assert marker appears once and _migrations has one entry; catches bug where applied set is not read/written correctly. Candidate Unit (adjacent lower): mock applied-migration lookup and assert executor not called; catches branch logic but misses real metadata persistence mismatch.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: Needs real transaction semantics plus runner stop-on-error orchestration across modules. Mocks can hide rollback behavior (MOCK-HIDE), so unit is too low. Rejected System: behavior text specifies DB migration failure/rollback in one run, not OS/subprocess/network behavior; local Postgres integration is sufficient. No LLM or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate Integration (chosen): first migration does partial writes then failing statement; second migration would write sentinel; assert first migration effects absent (rolled back) and sentinel absent (execution halted); catches missing transaction wrapping or continue-on-error bug. Candidate System (adjacent higher): run same via CLI subprocesses; catches process/exit-code wiring too, but those are not required by this behavior text.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires concurrent execution from separate processes. In-process integration fakes cannot faithfully reproduce inter-process race/locking timing; REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both force System. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM output and no multi-agent AI pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate System (chosen): launch two runner subprocesses simultaneously against same DB and same migrations; assert each migration's side effect exists exactly once and _migrations has single record per version; catches race where both processes apply same migration. Candidate Integration (adjacent lower): invoke two runner instances in-process with mocked locks; may pass while real process-level contention still duplicates execution.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the _migrations table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: This is initialization ordering across schema-bootstrap and migration-apply modules on real DB state. Unit tests can verify call order in isolation but miss true first-run DB preconditions. Rejected System: no requirement for subprocess/OS/network failure mode beyond local DB integration. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Candidate Integration (chosen): start with fresh test DB lacking _migrations; run runner with one migration; assert _migrations exists and migration applied successfully, with no relation-missing error; catches bug where apply starts before metadata bootstrap. Candidate Unit (adjacent lower): mock bootstrap and apply functions and assert invocation order; catches orchestration branch bug but misses real first-run schema creation failures.
